description: Processes each node independently and sums up the output.

parameterGroups.label: Crossover Frequencies
parameterGroups.rationale: Two crossover frequency parameters define the boundaries between three frequency bands. Band 1 is the low-mid crossover, Band 2 is the mid-high crossover. Each parameter routes to three LR filters (one per band) at that crossover point.

gaps.question: freq_split3 has 3 bands and 2 crossover parameters vs freq_split2's 2 bands and 1 crossover. Each crossover parameter connects to 3 LR filters (lr1_N, lr2_N, lr3_N -- one per band). Is the internal logic identical to freq_split2 except for the additional band and crossover, with the middle band using bandpass behaviour (HP from lower crossover, LP from upper crossover)?
gaps.context: If identical in structure, the doc can reference the base variant and note only the band count and parameter count difference.

gaps.question: In a 3-band split, the middle band needs both a high-pass (from crossover 1) and a low-pass (from crossover 2). What Type values are assigned to the LR filters in band2 to achieve bandpass behaviour?
gaps.context: Middle bands in multi-way crossovers require two filter stages. Users need to understand how this is implemented.

notes: Variant of freq_splitN with N=3. Adds one band and one crossover parameter vs the base. Each Band parameter now connects to 3 LR filters (one per band). The lr4_3.png image directly depicts this 3-band variant. Band 1 connects to lr1_1, lr2_1, lr3_1; Band 2 connects to lr1_2, lr2_2, lr3_2. The naming pattern suggests lrX_Y where X is the band index and Y is the crossover index.
